channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: It looks like Microsoft is forcing its controversial Windows 11 24H2 update on people and (plot twist!) it might be for the best
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows 11 24H2 update is rolling out automatically to eligible PCs, promising improved performance and new features. Some bugs persist and ‘Pause’ option gives you the chance to delay.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to manage photos, videos on new laptop with limited space
item.description: With limited hard drive space, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers these tips for storing large video files and photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fresh Nvidia RTX 5090 and 5080 stock rumors again suggest that next-gen GPUs will not be easy to buy
item.description: Latest rumors on RTX 5000 stock have me even more worried that it’ll be a frustrating scramble to buy these GPUs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: After 10 years of Apple Watch, the company reveals the one thing it got wrong about its users
item.description: Apple has revealed it expected sporty Apple Watch users to embrace exercise when they launched Apple Watch, while less sporty users would focus on other things. Turns out they were wrong.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: The best Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra model could be exclusive to Asia
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra could be stuck with 12GB of RAM in much of the world, and there's growing evidence that its S Pen will lack Bluetooth.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Retorno rápido de la inversión frente a innovación: los objetivos contrapuestos de los CIO
item.description: La estrategia de los CIO en materia de inteligencia artificial (IA) es hoy una historia de agendas contrapuestas: mejoras rápidas de la productividad, por un lado, e innovaciones a largo plazo que cambian las reglas del juego, por otro.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: From devops to CTO: 5 things to start doing now
item.description: I was promoted to CTO in my late twenties, and while it is common to see young CTOs leading startups these days, it was unusual in the ’90s. I was far less experienced back then, and still developing my business acumen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI security: establishing the first and last layer of defense
item.description: With rapid AI adoption, businesses need new methods to protect their data. Network segmentation is the key to establishing a first and last line of defense.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Man admits to setting fire to building housing Wisconsin rep's office over TikTok ban
item.description: A man has admitted to setting fire to a building housing a House member from Wisconsin’s office over “recent talks of a TikTok ban,” according to local police.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Alabama refuge is a paradise for birders and thousands of migrating sandhill cranes
item.description: Flooded cornfields near the Tennessee River in northern Alabama are a paradise for birders who flock to see tens of thousands of migrating sandhill cranes every winter.
item.dc:creator: Kristin M. Hall, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: China gains ground
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How Apple is in the race for workplace AI
item.description: In a few years, every new employee entering the workforce will already have become accustomed to using AI to solve problems and help with tasks – and they’re going to want the same tools at work as those they use at home.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google rolls out Vertex AI RAG Engine
item.description: Google has formally introduced Vertex AI RAG Engine, a developer tool aimed at streamlining the complex process of retrieving relevant information from a knowledge base and feeding it to an LLM (large language model).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One reason the U.S. government is so spooked by TikTok: AI
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here. The real reason the U.S. government wants to ban a ByteDance-owned TikTok TikTok could shut down in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden cyber EO seeks proof of security from software vendors
item.description: The new cybersecurity executive order seeks to put teeth behind the secure software development standards that federal vendors are supposed to be following. The post Biden cyber EO seeks proof of security from software vendors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: From Safety to Accountability: The Game-Changing Impact of Kazakhstan’s Surveillance System
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Joe Biden going out same way as Aaron Rodgers
item.description: Bill O'Reilly believes outgoing president Joe Biden is "going out the same way" as New York Jets’ starting quarterback Aaron Rodgers.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Fox will stream next month's Super Bowl for free on Tubi
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fox will stream the Super Bowl on Tubi for the first time, giving streamers and cord cutters a chance to watch the big game on Feb. 9 for free. Tubi was acquired by Fox Corporation in 2020. It is a free, ad-supported streaming service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Qualcomm purloins Intel’s chief Xeon designer with eyes toward data center development
item.description: If Intel was hoping for a turnaround in 2025, it will have to wait at least a little bit longer. The chief architect for Intel’s Xeon server processors has defected to chip rival Qualcomm, which is making yet another run at entering the data center market.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft adds ‘pay-as-you-go’ access for AI agents with M365 Copilot Chat
item.description: Microsoft has added a “pay-as-you-go” option to access AI agents alongside its free M365 Copilot Chat plan.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What the tech industry wants from the Trump administration and Congress
item.description: The technology industry is calling on the incoming Trump administration and Congress to create the necessary tax and regulatory environment. The post What the tech industry wants from the Trump administration and Congress first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Get ready to experience thrill of eVTOL racing
item.description: The Jetson One eVTOL showcases air racing potential, ushering in a new aviation era. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the race for eVTOL supremacy is just beginning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
